notes: Direct deps: root -> core (workspace/local), root -> web (workspace/local), core -> zod (registry), web -> hono (registry). The web -> core edge is workspace:* — encoded as local/editable.
No transitive deps: hono and zod have zero runtime dependencies, so there are no nodes beyond direct edges.

Mend may scan each workspace member as a sub-project and report per-member trees in addition to or instead of the unified root tree. If Mend reports separate sub-trees, core should show 1 direct (zod) and web should show 2 directs (core as local, hono as registry).

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver. There is no 'bun' entry in Mend's install-tool list — scanSettings.versioning cannot pin a Bun toolchain. Detection relies entirely on static bun.lock parsing.

workspace:* cross-reference (web -> core): Mend must resolve this to source='local' using the workspaces section of bun.lock, NOT as an npm registry lookup. If Mend emits source='registry' for core, or reports a 404 / ghost dependency, that is the workspace:* protocol failure documented in BUN_COVERAGE_PLAN.md §4.

The bun.lock packages section intentionally omits 'core' — local workspace members are NOT listed in the packages object. A Mend parser that expects all deps to appear in packages will silently drop 'core' from the web sub-tree. That is a false negative.

Mend may report workspace members as separate projects (sub-scans) rather than as nodes in a unified root tree. Either behavior is acceptable; the comparator should tolerate both representations. The workspace_edges array above encodes the expected cross-reference regardless of representation.

If Mend falls back to npm ls or filesystem scan (because bun.lock is not recognized), the workspace: protocol will not be understood and the web -> core edge will be dropped. Expected tree encodes the correct resolved graph; comparator should flag any dropped edge.
